extraction_candidates.name: Meta-Memory Skills Framework
extraction_candidates.description: The core concept: skills about HOW to remember rather than WHAT to remember. Skill-conditioned memory construction where a controller selects relevant skills per text span and an executor applies them in one pass. 15 evolved skill files demonstrate the output.
extraction_candidates.notes: Concept is language-agnostic. The 5-section Markdown skill format (Description, Purpose, When to Use, How to Apply, Constraints) is directly portable. Read the paper for full framework.

extraction_candidates.name: Skill Evolution Loop (Designer)
extraction_candidates.description: 3-stage LLM-driven skill evolution: (1) failure mining with clustering, (2) analysis + reflection loop with self-critique, (3) refinement producing concrete skill mutations. Triggered periodically, not on every step.
extraction_candidates.notes: Designer prompts are the most portable artifact. The two-stage analysis+reflection pattern is applicable to any self-improving system. Python-specific PPO integration less portable.

extraction_candidates.name: Skill Bank Markdown Format
extraction_candidates.description: Standardized 5-section Markdown format for serialized memory skills: Title, Description, Purpose, When to Use, How to Apply, Constraints + Action type declaration. 15 examples across 2 domains.
extraction_candidates.notes: Direct copy. Compare against sonash-v0 SKILL.md format. Could inform memory skill file format for JASON-OS.

extraction_candidates.name: Designer Prompt Templates
extraction_candidates.description: Two-stage prompts: Stage 1 classifies failures as storage/retrieval/quality failures with structured JSON output. Stage 1b is a reflection loop critiquing the analysis. Stage 2 produces concrete skill mutations. ~18KB of prompt engineering.
extraction_candidates.notes: Pure text prompts — maximally portable. The failure classification taxonomy (storage vs retrieval vs quality) is a useful framework for any memory system debugging.

extraction_candidates.name: Dual-Embedding Memory Bank
extraction_candidates.description: Each memory item carries two embeddings: a retriever embedding (for QA/search) and a state encoder embedding (for the PPO controller's input). Enables different views of the same memory for different consumers.
extraction_candidates.notes: Concept portable, implementation tied to FAISS + PyTorch. The dual-view pattern is interesting for any multi-consumer memory system.

extraction_candidates.name: Operation Templates with Meta-Info
extraction_candidates.description: Seed skill definitions with usage tracking: usage_count, avg_reward, recent_rewards (deque), recent_usage_ema, created_at, last_modified. Enables data-driven skill selection and evolution.
extraction_candidates.notes: The meta_info tracking pattern (usage + reward + EMA) is applicable to any skill/tool selection system. Portable as a design pattern.
